summary: Tesla  has benefited from higher oil prices in the past, so it might seem odd that crude is down sharply and the electric-vehicle maker’s stock is up.  Benchmark oil prices were down more than 13% in early trading, below $95 a barrel.  Hopes of an end to fighting in Iran are responsible for the moves.

headline: Tesla Stock Jumps Despite Slumping Oil Prices. Why It’s Getting an Iran Bump.
summary: Tesla  has benefited from higher oil prices in the past, so it might seem odd that crude is down sharply and the electric-vehicle maker’s stock is up.  Shares of the Elon Musk-led company were up more than 4% in premarket trading and gained 2.6% in early trading to $355.57, while the  and  were up 2.6% and 2.9%, respectively.  Benchmark oil prices were down more 16% in early trading, below $92 a barrel.

content.title: Tesla Stock Jumps Despite Slumping Oil Prices. Why It’s Getting an Iran Bump.
content.summary: Tesla  has benefited from higher oil prices in the past, so it might seem odd that crude is down sharply and the electric-vehicle maker’s stock is up.  Shares of the Elon Musk-led company were up more than 4% in premarket trading and gained 2.6% in early trading to $355.57, while the  and  were up 2.6% and 2.9%, respectively.  Benchmark oil prices were down more 16% in early trading, below $92 a barrel.

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Surges 1,200 Points As Fed Minutes Loom; Alphabet Soars (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes held the bulk of their hefty gains Wednesday afternoon after President Donald Trump suspended Iran attacks for two weeks while Tehran agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz.  Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's minutes from the March rate-setting meeting are due on the stock market today.  In early afternoon trade, the Dow Jones Industrial Average jumped 2.7%, or more than 1,200 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

content.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Surges 1,200 Points As Fed Minutes Loom; Alphabet Soars (Live Coverage)
content.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes held the bulk of their hefty gains Wednesday afternoon after President Donald Trump suspended Iran attacks for two weeks while Tehran agreed to open the Strait of Hormuz.  Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve's minutes from the March rate-setting meeting are due on the stock market today.  In early afternoon trade, the Dow Jones Industrial Average jumped 2.7%, or more than 1,200 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

headline: Dow spikes by over 1,300, oil prices retreat in ceasefire rally
summary: All three of the major indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed Wednesday's session with colossal gains — the Dow Jones Industrial Average skyrocketed by over 1,320 points (2.85%) and the Nasdaq Composite surged by nearly 620 points (2.8%) — after President Trump announced that Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire last night. Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre takes a closer look at the trading day's biggest sector gainers.

content.title: Dow spikes by over 1,300, oil prices retreat in ceasefire rally
content.description:
All three of the major indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed Wednesday's session with colossal gains — the Dow Jones Industrial Average skyrocketed by over 1,320 points (2.85%) and the Nasdaq Composite surged by nearly 620 points (2.8%) — after President Trump announced that Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire last night.

Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre takes a closer look at the trading day's biggest sector gainers.
content.summary: All three of the major indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed Wednesday's session with colossal gains — the Dow Jones Industrial Average skyrocketed by over 1,320 points (2.85%) and the Nasdaq Composite surged by nearly 620 points (2.8%) — after President Trump announced that Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire last night. Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre takes a closer look at the trading day's biggest sector gainers.

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow Rallies 1,300 Points Amid Iran Ceasefire; This Chip Stock Breaks Out (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes finished Wednesday's trading day with solid gains after a ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.  Monolithic Power and IBD Leaderboard name Comfort Systems were among several breakouts on the stock market today.  On Wednesday, the Dow scored a 2.9% gain or more than 1,300 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

Monolithic Power and IBD Leaderboard name Comfort Systems were among several breakouts on the stock market today.  On Wednesday, the Dow scored a 2.9% gain or more than 1,300 points, while the S&P 500 advanced 2.5%.

headline: Tesla Faces $43.9 Billion Free Cash Flow Swing As 2026 Outlook Turns Negative
summary: Consensus 2026 free cash flow shifted from $38.8 billion to negative $5.1 billion, even as valuation multiples expanded and deliveries missed forecasts.

headline: Tesla Stock Just Can’t Win. Its Iran Bump Faded.
summary: Tesla  has benefited from higher oil prices in the past, so it seemed odd that crude was down sharply and the electric-vehicle maker’s stock was up.  Oil prices helped most stocks.  Hopes of an end to fighting in Iran are responsible for the oil move.
